clusters.title: Trump 'unsatisfied' with Iran peace proposal
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump 'unsatisfied' with Iran peace proposal
bullet_summary: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication"
Reported by The Age, ABC News Australia

items.title: Trump 'unsatisfied' with Iran peace proposal
items.standfirst: Iran is seeking a US retreat from the Strait of Hormuz in its latest peace proposal.
items.content: Iran is seeking a US retreat from the Strait of Hormuz in its latest peace proposal.

items.title: Trump says Iran ‘negotiating on fumes’ as Israel ramps up conflict in Lebanon
items.standfirst: The US president said he was “not satisfied” by negotiations with Iran as the White House labelled an Iranian report on a draft deal as a “complete fabrication”, damping expectations for an imminent breakthrough.
items.content: The US president said he was “not satisfied” by negotiations with Iran as the White House labelled an Iranian report on a draft deal as a “complete fabrication”, damping expectations for an imminent breakthrough.

items.title: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran peace deal proposals
items.standfirst: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".
items.content: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".

items.title: Legal action filed over federal government's land clearing decision
items.standfirst: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.
items.content: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

items.title: Brothers torn apart by Stolen Generations policies share their story
items.standfirst: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.
items.content: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.
